highlights.text: I wrote yesterday in my newsletter sent out at 3pm: “Bear case tomorrow: Begins below 5843.
As always, I’d need to see ES test 5843 and try and put in a Failed Breakdown of todays 5846 low.
Last night after 4pm we saw exactly this: We tested 5843, put in a Failed Breakdown of yesterdays 5846 low, rallied to 5860, then after 6pm last night, ES lost the cluster of support.
Every support will fail until we get the routine post “elevator down” short squeeze”.

In today’s newsletter I’ll talk this, I’ll discuss the setup that triggered last nights short (its my rarest trade type: The Breakdown Short), and contrast that with my most common setup, the Failed Breakdown.

post_title: Biggest Crash For SPX In 2 Months. Buyable Dip? Or More Red Ahead? Nov 1 Plan
